the-euler-identity.txt.title: The Euler Identity
the-euler-identity.txt.content:
Euler's Identity: e^(iπ) + 1 = 0

Often called the most beautiful equation in mathematics,
Euler's identity connects five fundamental constants:

  e  — Euler's number (2.71828...), base of natural logarithms
  i  — The imaginary unit, where i² = -1
  π  — Pi (3.14159...), the ratio of a circle's circumference
       to its diameter
  1  — The multiplicative identity
  0  — The additive identity

The identity is a special case of Euler's formula:

  e^(ix) = cos(x) + i·sin(x)

When x = π, we get:
  e^(iπ) = cos(π) + i·sin(π)
  e^(iπ) = -1 + 0
  e^(iπ) + 1 = 0

This elegant result is often the first glimpse into the
profound unity of mathematics for many students.

photography-tips.txt.title: Photography Tips for Beginners
photography-tips.txt.content:
Quick Photography Tips
=====================

1. The Rule of Thirds
   Imagine a 3x3 grid over your frame. Place key subjects
   along the lines or at their intersections for a more
   balanced composition.

2. Natural Light is Your Friend
   The golden hours (just after sunrise and before sunset)
   provide warm, diffused light that flatters most subjects.
   Avoid harsh midday sun when shadows are too strong.

3. Get Closer
   Fill the frame with your subject. Most beginning
   photographers leave too much empty space around
   their subject.

4. Keep the Horizon Level
   A tilted horizon is distracting. Use your camera's
   grid overlay to keep it straight.

5. Experiment with Angles
   Don't just shoot from eye level. Try crouching down,
   climbing up, or tilting the camera for more dynamic
   perspectives.

6. Focus on the Eyes
   For portraits, the eyes should always be in sharp
   focus. They are the most important element.

Equipment:
- Any camera is better than no camera
- A 50mm f/1.8 lens is the best value in photography
- A tripod unlocks long exposures and sharper images

Remember: the best camera is the one you have with you.

about-samuelphan.txt.title: About samuelphan.com
about-samuelphan.txt.content:
Welcome to samuelphan.com!

This site is a nostalgic recreation of the Windows XP
desktop experience, built with pure HTML, CSS, and
vanilla JavaScript.

No frameworks. No build tools. Just the web.

Inspired by:
- Microsoft Windows XP (2001-2014)
- The blissful simplicity of early 2000s web design
- XP.css by botoxparty for the Luna theme components

Features:
- Fully functional window manager (drag, resize, min/max/close)
- Start menu with flyout submenus
- Taskbar with real-time clock
- Classic Bliss desktop wallpaper recreation
- Notepad application with real content

This project is a playground for web development and
a love letter to an operating system that defined a
generation.

Built by Samuel Phan
(c) 2026 - All rights reserved
